in a prior session, we had created a repo and it might be in temp files for prior claude. we were going to deploy a new static website to https://github.com/Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us through commoncloud.git github actions to deploy to my aws lightsail vm the same way commoncloud.git/04-docker-server please find the repo and let's continue iterating the design. i am viewing the draft on http://localhost:8080

content.text: I'll start by exploring to find the existing repo and understand the current state of things.

content.content:
1→# ===========================================
     2→# Common Cloud - Release Workflow
     3→# ===========================================
     4→# Creates releases and generates changelog
     5→# Triggered manually or on version tags
     6→
     7→name: Release
     8→
     9→on:
    10→  push:
    11→    tags:
    12→      - "v*"
    13→  workflow_dispatch:
    14→    inputs:
    15→      version:
    16→        description: "Release version (e.g., v1.1.0)"
    17→        required: true
    18→        type: string
    19→
    20→jobs:
    21→  # ===========================================
    22→  # Validate Before Release
    23→  # ===========================================
    24→  validate:
    25→    name: Pre-Release Validation
    26→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    27→    permissions:
    28→      contents: read
    29→
    30→    steps:
    31→      - name: Checkout code
    32→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    33→
    34→      - name: Setup Terraform
    35→        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
    36→        with:
    37→          terraform_version: "1.6.0"
    38→
    39→      - name: Validate Terraform
    40→        run: |
    41→          cd 00-terraform
    42→          terraform init -backend=false
    43→          terraform validate
    44→
    45→      - name: Validate Docker Compose
    46→        run: |
    47→          cat > 04-docker-server/.env << 'EOF'
    48→          N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER=test
    49→          N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD=test
    50→          N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY=test-key-32-characters-long-xxx
    51→          STATAMIC_APP_KEY=base64:test-key-for-ci-validation
    52→          BILLING_DB_PASSWORD=test
    53→          TZ=UTC
    54→          EOF
    55→          cd 04-docker-server
    56→          docker compose config --quiet
    57→
    58→  # ===========================================
    59→  # Create GitHub Release
    60→  # ===========================================
    61→  release:
    62→    name: Create Release
    63→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    64→    needs: validate
    65→    permissions:
    66→      contents: write
    67→
    68→    steps:
    69→      - name: Checkout code
    70→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    71→        with:
    72→          fetch-depth: 0
    73→
    74→      - name: Generate changelog
    75→        id: changelog
    76→        run: |
    77→          # Get previous tag
    78→          PREV_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 HEAD^ 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    79→
    80→          if [ -z "$PREV_TAG" ]; then
    81→            echo "changelog=Initial release" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    82→          else
    83→            CHANGES=$(git log ${PREV_TAG}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --no-merges)
    84→            echo "changelog<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    85→            echo "$CHANGES" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    86→            echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    87→          fi
    88→
    89→      - name: Create Release
    90→        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
    91→        with:
    92→          name: Common Cloud ${{ github.ref_name || inputs.version }}
    93→          body: |
    94→            ## What's Changed
    95→
    96→            ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.changelog }}
    97→
    98→            ## Installation
    99→
   100→            See [INSTRUCTIONS-01-AWS.md](docs/INSTRUCTIONS-01-AWS.md) to get started.
   101→
   102→            ## Upgrade Notes
   103→
   104→            ```bash
   105→            # Pull latest changes
   106→            git pull origin main
   107→
   108→            # Update Docker services
   109→            make update
   110→
   111→            # Verify health
   112→            make health-verbose
   113→            ```
   114→          draft: false
   115→          prerelease: false
   116→          generate_release_notes: true
   117→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.content:
1→# ===========================================
     2→# Common Cloud - Documentation Checks
     3→# ===========================================
     4→# Validates markdown files and checks links
     5→# Runs on documentation changes
     6→
     7→name: Documentation
     8→
     9→on:
    10→  push:
    11→    paths:
    12→      - "**.md"
    13→      - "docs/**"
    14→  pull_request:
    15→    paths:
    16→      - "**.md"
    17→      - "docs/**"
    18→
    19→jobs:
    20→  # ===========================================
    21→  # Markdown Linting
    22→  # ===========================================
    23→  markdown-lint:
    24→    name: Markdown Lint
    25→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    26→    permissions:
    27→      contents: read
    28→
    29→    steps:
    30→      - name: Checkout code
    31→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    32→
    33→      - name: Lint Markdown files
    34→        uses: DavidAnson/markdownlint-cli2-action@v16
    35→        with:
    36→          globs: |
    37→            **/*.md
    38→            !00-terraform/.terraform/**/*.md
    39→            !**/node_modules/**/*.md
    40→            !dist/**/*.md
    41→            !build/**/*.md
    42→          config-file: ".markdownlint.json"
    43→        continue-on-error: false
    44→
    45→  # ===========================================
    46→  # Link Checking
    47→  # ===========================================
    48→  link-check:
    49→    name: Check Links
    50→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    51→    permissions:
    52→      contents: read
    53→
    54→    steps:
    55→      - name: Checkout code
    56→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    57→
    58→      - name: Check markdown links
    59→        uses: gaurav-nelson/github-action-markdown-link-check@v1
    60→        with:
    61→          use-quiet-mode: "yes"
    62→          config-file: ".github/workflows/mlc_config.json"
    63→        continue-on-error: true
    64→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→# ===========================================
     2→# CLAUDE NOTES - READ BEFORE PROCEEDING
     3→# ===========================================
     4→# DEPLOYMENT STATUS: ✅ DEPLOYED on aws-docker server
     5→# Last deployed: December 20, 2025
     6→# Last updated: February 6, 2026
     7→#
     8→# LIVE CONTAINERS (verified Feb 2026):
     9→# - nginx-proxy, appsmith, n8n, statamic, redis, billing-db
    10→# - vvveb, splunk, datadog-agent
    11→# - appflowy-cloud, appflowy-gotrue, appflowy-postgres, appflowy-redis, appflowy-minio
    12→# - anytype, snikket, snikket-portal
    13→#
    14→# PRIVACY BY DESIGN:
    15→# - All services run on isolated bridge network (no direct internet access)
    16→# - Only nginx exposes ports 80/443 externally
    17→# - Email via Migadu (privacy-focused, Swiss-based)
    18→# - Datadog agent for infrastructure monitoring (opt-in)
    19→#
    20→# SECURITY HARDENING:
    21→# - [x] Health checks on all containers (auto-restart unhealthy)
    22→# - [x] Read-only config mounts (:ro) where possible
    23→# - [x] No privileged containers
    24→# - [x] Internal network isolation
    25→# - [x] Resource limits (mem_limit, cpus) on all containers
    26→# - [x] security_opt: no-new-privileges on all containers
    27→# - [x] Rate limiting configured in nginx
    28→#
    29→# BEFORE MODIFYING:
    30→# 1. SSH to server: ssh aws-docker
    31→# 2. Backup current: cp ~/commoncloud/docker-compose.yml ~/commoncloud/docker-compose.yml.bak
    32→# 3. Test changes locally first if possible
    33→# 4. After changes: sudo docker compose up -d && sudo docker compose ps
    34→#
    35→# DOMAIN NOTE: This file uses cloudpublica.org domain
    36→# The INSTRUCTIONS.md matches cloudpublica.org domain - update if domain changes
    37→# ===========================================
    38→
    39→services:
    40→  # ===========================================
    41→  # NGINX Reverse Proxy
    42→  # ===========================================
    43→  nginx:
    44→    image: nginx:alpine
    45→    container_name: nginx-proxy
    46→    restart: unless-stopped
    47→    ports:
    48→      - "80:80"
    49→      - "443:443"
    50→    volumes:
    51→      - ./nginx/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
    52→      - ./nginx/conf.d:/etc/nginx/conf.d:ro
    53→      - ./nginx/certs:/etc/nginx/certs:ro
    54→      - ./nginx/static:/var/www/anytype:ro
    55→      - ./certbot/www:/var/www/certbot:ro
    56→      - ./sites/gifteddreamers.org:/var/www/gifteddreamers.org:ro
    57→    depends_on:
    58→      - appsmith
    59→      - n8n
    60→      - statamic
    61→      - vvveb
    62→      - splunk
    63→      - appflowy-cloud
    64→      - anytype
    65→      - snikket
    66→      - linkstack-mcp
    67→    networks:
    68→      - commoncloud
    69→    labels:
    70→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
    71→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=nginx-proxy"
    72→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
    73→    # Security hardening
    74→    security_opt:
    75→      - no-new-privileges:true
    76→    deploy:
    77→      resources:
    78→        limits:
    79→          memory: 256M
    80→          cpus: '0.5'
    81→        reservations:
    82→          memory: 64M
    83→
    84→  # ===========================================
    85→  # APPSMITH - Low-Code Platform
    86→  # hq.cloudpublica.org
    87→  # ===========================================
    88→  appsmith:
    89→    image: appsmith/appsmith-ce:latest
    90→    container_name: appsmith
    91→    restart: unless-stopped
    92→    volumes:
    93→      - appsmith_data:/appsmith-stacks
    94→    environment:
    95→      - APPSMITH_MAIL_ENABLED=true
    96→      - APPSMITH_MAIL_HOST=smtp.migadu.com
    97→      - APPSMITH_MAIL_PORT=587
    98→      - APPSMITH_MAIL_USERNAME=${APPSMITH_MAIL_USER:-noreply@cloudpublica.org}
    99→      - APPSMITH_MAIL_PASSWORD=${APPSMITH_MAIL_PASSWORD:-}
   100→      - APPSMITH_MAIL_FROM=noreply@cloudpublica.org
   101→    networks:
   102→      - commoncloud
   103→    labels:
   104→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   105→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=appsmith"
   106→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   107→    healthcheck:
   108→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/api/v1/health"]
   109→      interval: 30s
   110→      timeout: 10s
   111→      retries: 3
   112→    # Security hardening
   113→    security_opt:
   114→      - no-new-privileges:true
   115→    deploy:
   116→      resources:
   117→        limits:
   118→          memory: 4G
   119→          cpus: '2.0'
   120→        reservations:
   121→          memory: 1G
   122→
   123→  # ===========================================
   124→  # N8N - Workflow Automation
   125→  # n8n.cloudpublica.org
   126→  # ===========================================
   127→  n8n:
   128→    image: n8nio/n8n:latest
   129→    container_name: n8n
   130→    restart: unless-stopped
   131→    volumes:
   132→      - n8n_data:/home/node/.n8n
   133→    environment:
   134→      - N8N_HOST=n8n.cloudpublica.org
   135→      - N8N_PORT=5678
   136→      - N8N_PROTOCOL=https
   137→      - WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/
   138→      - N8N_BASIC_AUTH_ACTIVE=true
   139→      - N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER=${N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER}
   140→      - N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD=${N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD}
   141→      - N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY=${N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY}
   142→      - GENERIC_TIMEZONE=${TZ:-America/New_York}
   143→      - TZ=${TZ:-America/New_York}
   144→      # Email configuration (uses Migadu SMTP)
   145→      - N8N_EMAIL_MODE=smtp
   146→      - N8N_SMTP_HOST=smtp.migadu.com
   147→      - N8N_SMTP_PORT=587
   148→      - N8N_SMTP_USER=${N8N_SMTP_USER:-noreply@cloudpublica.org}
   149→      - N8N_SMTP_PASS=${N8N_SMTP_PASS:-}
   150→      - N8N_SMTP_SENDER=noreply@cloudpublica.org
   151→      # MCP Server for Claude Desktop integration
   152→      - N8N_MCP_SERVER_ENABLED=true
   153→      # Enable community packages as AI tools
   154→      - N8N_COMMUNITY_PACKAGES_ALLOW_TOOL_USAGE=true
   155→    networks:
   156→      - commoncloud
   157→    labels:
   158→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   159→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=n8n"
   160→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   161→    healthcheck:
   162→      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:5678/healthz"]
   163→      interval: 30s
   164→      timeout: 10s
   165→      retries: 3
   166→    # Security hardening
   167→    security_opt:
   168→      - no-new-privileges:true
   169→    deploy:
   170→      resources:
   171→        limits:
   172→          memory: 2G
   173→          cpus: '1.0'
   174→        reservations:
   175→          memory: 512M
   176→
   177→  # ===========================================
   178→  # STATAMIC - Flat-File CMS (using official starter kit)
   179→  # base.cloudpublica.org
   180→  # ===========================================
   181→  statamic:
   182→    build:
   183→      context: ./statamic
   184→      dockerfile: Dockerfile
   185→    container_name: statamic
   186→    restart: unless-stopped
   187→    volumes:
   188→      - statamic_data:/var/www/html
   189→    environment:
   190→      - APP_NAME="Common Cloud Knowledge Base"
   191→      - APP_ENV=production
   192→      - APP_KEY=${STATAMIC_APP_KEY}
   193→      - APP_DEBUG=false
   194→      - APP_URL=https://base.cloudpublica.org
   195→      - LOG_CHANNEL=stack
   196→      - MAIL_MAILER=smtp
   197→      - MAIL_HOST=smtp.migadu.com
   198→      - MAIL_PORT=587
   199→      - MAIL_USERNAME=${STATAMIC_MAIL_USER:-noreply@cloudpublica.org}
   200→      - MAIL_PASSWORD=${STATAMIC_MAIL_PASS:-}
   201→      - MAIL_ENCRYPTION=tls
   202→      - MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS=noreply@cloudpublica.org
   203→      - MAIL_FROM_NAME="Common Cloud"
   204→    networks:
   205→      - commoncloud
   206→    labels:
   207→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   208→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=statamic"
   209→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   210→    healthcheck:
   211→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost"]
   212→      interval: 30s
   213→      timeout: 10s
   214→      retries: 3
   215→    # Security hardening
   216→    security_opt:
   217→      - no-new-privileges:true
   218→    deploy:
   219→      resources:
   220→        limits:
   221→          memory: 1G
   222→          cpus: '1.0'
   223→        reservations:
   224→          memory: 256M
   225→
   226→  # ===========================================
   227→  # REDIS - Caching (optional, improves performance)
   228→  # ===========================================
   229→  redis:
   230→    image: redis:alpine
   231→    container_name: redis
   232→    restart: unless-stopped
   233→    volumes:
   234→      - redis_data:/data
   235→    networks:
   236→      - commoncloud
   237→    labels:
   238→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   239→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=redis"
   240→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   241→    healthcheck:
   242→      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
   243→      interval: 30s
   244→      timeout: 10s
   245→      retries: 3
   246→    # Security hardening
   247→    security_opt:
   248→      - no-new-privileges:true
   249→    deploy:
   250→      resources:
   251→        limits:
   252→          memory: 256M
   253→          cpus: '0.5'
   254→        reservations:
   255→          memory: 64M
   256→
   257→  # ===========================================
   258→  # POSTGRESQL - Billing Database
   259→  # Used by n8n for subscription/payment data
   260→  # ===========================================
   261→  billing-db:
   262→    image: postgres:15-alpine
   263→    container_name: billing-db
   264→    restart: unless-stopped
   265→    environment:
   266→      - POSTGRES_DB=billing
   267→      - POSTGRES_USER=billing
   268→      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${BILLING_DB_PASSWORD}
   269→    volumes:
   270→      - billing_db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   271→      - ./billing-schema.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/01-schema.sql:ro
   272→    networks:
   273→      - commoncloud
   274→    labels:
   275→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   276→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=billing-db"
   277→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   278→    healthcheck:
   279→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U billing -d billing"]
   280→      interval: 30s
   281→      timeout: 10s
   282→      retries: 3
   283→    # Security hardening
   284→    security_opt:
   285→      - no-new-privileges:true
   286→    deploy:
   287→      resources:
   288→        limits:
   289→          memory: 512M
   290→          cpus: '0.5'
   291→        reservations:
   292→          memory: 128M
   293→
   294→  # ===========================================
   295→  # VVVEB - Website Builder
   296→  # vvveb.cloudpublica.org
   297→  # ===========================================
   298→  vvveb:
   299→    image: vvveb/vvvebcms
   300→    container_name: vvveb
   301→    restart: unless-stopped
   302→    networks:
   303→      - commoncloud
   304→    labels:
   305→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   306→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=vvveb"
   307→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   308→    # Security hardening
   309→    security_opt:
   310→      - no-new-privileges:true
   311→    deploy:
   312→      resources:
   313→        limits:
   314→          memory: 512M
   315→          cpus: '0.5'
   316→        reservations:
   317→          memory: 128M
   318→
   319→  # ===========================================
   320→  # SPLUNK ENTERPRISE - Log Analysis & Security
   321→  # logs.cloudpublica.org
   322→  # ===========================================
   323→  splunk:
   324→    image: splunk/splunk:latest
   325→    container_name: splunk
   326→    restart: unless-stopped
   327→    ports:
   328→      - "8000:8000"   # Web UI (internal only, proxied via nginx)
   329→      - "8088:8088"   # HTTP Event Collector
   330→      - "9997:9997"   # Receiving port (forwarders)
   331→    environment:
   332→      - SPLUNK_START_ARGS=--accept-license
   333→      - SPLUNK_GENERAL_TERMS=--accept-sgt-current-at-splunk-com
   334→      - SPLUNK_PASSWORD=${SPLUNK_PASSWORD}
   335→      - SPLUNK_LICENSE_URI=/tmp/license/splunk.lic
   336→    volumes:
   337→      - splunk_data:/opt/splunk/var
   338→      - splunk_etc:/opt/splunk/etc
   339→      - ./splunk-license/splunk.lic:/tmp/license/splunk.lic:ro
   340→    networks:
   341→      - commoncloud
   342→    labels:
   343→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   344→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=splunk"
   345→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   346→    healthcheck:
   347→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/services/server/health/splunkd/details"]
   348→      interval: 60s
   349→      timeout: 30s
   350→      retries: 3
   351→      start_period: 180s
   352→    # Note: Splunk requires privilege escalation during setup
   353→    deploy:
   354→      resources:
   355→        limits:
   356→          memory: 4G
   357→          cpus: '2.0'
   358→        reservations:
   359→          memory: 2G
   360→
   361→  # ===========================================
   362→  # DATADOG AGENT - Infrastructure Monitoring
   363→  # ===========================================
   364→  datadog-agent:
   365→    image: gcr.io/datadoghq/agent:latest
   366→    container_name: datadog-agent
   367→    restart: unless-stopped
   368→    environment:
   369→      - DD_API_KEY=${DD_API_KEY}
   370→      - DD_SITE=us5.datadoghq.com
   371→      - DD_HOSTNAME=commoncloud-docker
   372→      - DD_TAGS=env:production
   373→      - DD_LOGS_ENABLED=true
   374→      - DD_LOGS_CONFIG_CONTAINER_COLLECT_ALL=true
   375→      - DD_DOGSTATSD_NON_LOCAL_TRAFFIC=true
   376→      - DD_PROCESS_AGENT_ENABLED=true
   377→      - DD_APM_ENABLED=true
   378→    volumes:
   379→      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
   380→      - /proc/:/host/proc/:ro
   381→      - /sys/fs/cgroup/:/host/sys/fs/cgroup:ro
   382→      - /var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro
   383→    networks:
   384→      - commoncloud
   385→    healthcheck:
   386→      test: ["CMD", "agent", "health"]
   387→      interval: 30s
   388→      timeout: 10s
   389→      retries: 3
   390→    deploy:
   391→      resources:
   392→        limits:
   393→          memory: 512M
   394→          cpus: '0.5'
   395→        reservations:
   396→          memory: 256M
   397→
   398→  # ===========================================
   399→  # APPFLOWY CLOUD - Notion Alternative
   400→  # appflowy.cloudpublica.org
   401→  # ===========================================
   402→  appflowy-postgres:
   403→    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
   404→    container_name: appflowy-postgres
   405→    restart: unless-stopped
   406→    environment:
   407→      - POSTGRES_USER=appflowy
   408→      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${APPFLOWY_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
   409→      - POSTGRES_DB=appflowy
   410→    volumes:
   411→      - appflowy_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   412→    networks:
   413→      - commoncloud
   414→    labels:
   415→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   416→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=appflowy-postgres"
   417→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   418→    healthcheck:
   419→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U appflowy -d appflowy"]
   420→      interval: 30s
   421→      timeout: 10s
   422→      retries: 3
   423→    security_opt:
   424→      - no-new-privileges:true
   425→    deploy:
   426→      resources:
   427→        limits:
   428→          memory: 1G
   429→          cpus: '1.0'
   430→        reservations:
   431→          memory: 256M
   432→
   433→  appflowy-minio:
   434→    image: minio/minio
   435→    container_name: appflowy-minio
   436→    restart: unless-stopped
   437→    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
   438→    environment:
   439→      - MINIO_ROOT_USER=${APPFLOWY_MINIO_USER:-appflowy}
   440→      - MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD=${APPFLOWY_MINIO_PASSWORD}
   441→    volumes:
   442→      - appflowy_minio_data:/data
   443→    networks:
   444→      - commoncloud
   445→    labels:
   446→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   447→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=appflowy-minio"
   448→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   449→    healthcheck:
   450→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:9000/minio/health/live"]
   451→      interval: 30s
   452→      timeout: 10s
   453→      retries: 3
   454→    security_opt:
   455→      - no-new-privileges:true
   456→    deploy:
   457→      resources:
   458→        limits:
   459→          memory: 512M
   460→          cpus: '0.5'
   461→        reservations:
   462→          memory: 128M
   463→
   464→  appflowy-redis:
   465→    image: redis:alpine
   466→    container_name: appflowy-redis
   467→    restart: unless-stopped
   468→    volumes:
   469→      - appflowy_redis_data:/data
   470→    networks:
   471→      - commoncloud
   472→    labels:
   473→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   474→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=appflowy-redis"
   475→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   476→    healthcheck:
   477→      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
   478→      interval: 30s
   479→      timeout: 10s
   480→      retries: 3
   481→    security_opt:
   482→      - no-new-privileges:true
   483→    deploy:
   484→      resources:
   485→        limits:
   486→          memory: 256M
   487→          cpus: '0.5'
   488→        reservations:
   489→          memory: 64M
   490→
   491→  appflowy-gotrue:
   492→    image: appflowyinc/gotrue:latest
   493→    container_name: appflowy-gotrue
   494→    restart: unless-stopped
   495→    environment:
   496→      - GOTRUE_JWT_SECRET=${APPFLOWY_JWT_SECRET}
   497→      - GOTRUE_JWT_EXP=3600
   498→      - GOTRUE_DB_DRIVER=postgres
   499→      - DATABASE_URL=postgres://appflowy:${APPFLOWY_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@appflowy-postgres:5432/appflowy
   500→      - API_EXTERNAL_URL=https://appflowy.cloudpublica.org
   501→      - GOTRUE_SITE_URL=https://appflowy.cloudpublica.org
   502→      - GOTRUE_MAILER_AUTOCONFIRM=true
   503→      - GOTRUE_SMTP_HOST=smtp.migadu.com
   504→      - GOTRUE_SMTP_PORT=587
   505→      - GOTRUE_SMTP_USER=${APPFLOWY_SMTP_USER:-admin@cloudpublica.org}
   506→      - GOTRUE_SMTP_PASS=${APPFLOWY_SMTP_PASS}
   507→      - GOTRUE_SMTP_ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@cloudpublica.org
   508→      - GOTRUE_DISABLE_SIGNUP=false
   509→    depends_on:
   510→      appflowy-postgres:
   511→        condition: service_healthy
   512→    networks:
   513→      - commoncloud
   514→    labels:
   515→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   516→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=appflowy-gotrue"
   517→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   518→    healthcheck:
   519→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8081/health"]
   520→      interval: 30s
   521→      timeout: 10s
   522→      retries: 3
   523→    security_opt:
   524→      - no-new-privileges:true
   525→    deploy:
   526→      resources:
   527→        limits:
   528→          memory: 512M
   529→          cpus: '0.5'
   530→        reservations:
   531→          memory: 128M
   532→
   533→  appflowy-cloud:
   534→    image: appflowyinc/appflowy_cloud:latest
   535→    container_name: appflowy-cloud
   536→    restart: unless-stopped
   537→    environment:
   538→      - RUST_LOG=info
   539→      - APPFLOWY_ENVIRONMENT=production
   540→      - APPFLOWY_WEB_URL=https://appflowy.cloudpublica.org
   541→      - APPFLOWY_DATABASE_URL=postgres://appflowy:${APPFLOWY_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@appflowy-postgres:5432/appflowy
   542→      - APPFLOWY_REDIS_URI=redis://appflowy-redis:6379
   543→      - APPFLOWY_GOTRUE_BASE_URL=http://appflowy-gotrue:8081
   544→      - APPFLOWY_GOTRUE_JWT_SECRET=${APPFLOWY_JWT_SECRET}
   545→      - APPFLOWY_S3_USE_MINIO=true
   546→      - APPFLOWY_S3_MINIO_URL=http://appflowy-minio:9000
   547→      - APPFLOWY_S3_ACCESS_KEY=${APPFLOWY_MINIO_USER:-appflowy}
   548→      - APPFLOWY_S3_SECRET_KEY=${APPFLOWY_MINIO_PASSWORD}
   549→      - APPFLOWY_S3_BUCKET=appflowy
   550→      - APPFLOWY_S3_REGION=us-east-1
   551→      - APPFLOWY_INDEXER_ENABLED=false
   552→      - APPFLOWY_BASE_URL=https://appflowy.cloudpublica.org
   553→    depends_on:
   554→      appflowy-postgres:
   555→        condition: service_healthy
   556→      appflowy-redis:
   557→        condition: service_healthy
   558→      appflowy-gotrue:
   559→        condition: service_healthy
   560→      appflowy-minio:
   561→        condition: service_healthy
   562→    networks:
   563→      - commoncloud
   564→    labels:
   565→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   566→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=appflowy-cloud"
   567→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   568→    healthcheck:
   569→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/api/health"]
   570→      interval: 30s
   571→      timeout: 10s
   572→      retries: 3
   573→    security_opt:
   574→      - no-new-privileges:true
   575→    deploy:
   576→      resources:
   577→        limits:
   578→          memory: 2G
   579→          cpus: '1.0'
   580→        reservations:
   581→          memory: 512M
   582→
   583→  # ===========================================
   584→  # ANYTYPE - Knowledge Management
   585→  # anytype.cloudpublica.org
   586→  # ===========================================
   587→  # No web UI - desktop/mobile sync server only
   588→  # Clients connect via DRPC (33010) and QUIC (33020/udp)
   589→  anytype:
   590→    image: ghcr.io/grishy/any-sync-bundle:latest
   591→    container_name: anytype
   592→    restart: unless-stopped
   593→    ports:
   594→      - "33010:33010"  # DRPC sync protocol
   595→      - "33020:33020/udp"  # QUIC protocol
   596→    environment:
   597→      - ANY_SYNC_BUNDLE_INIT_EXTERNAL_ADDRS=anytype.cloudpublica.org
   598→    volumes:
   599→      - anytype_data:/data
   600→    networks:
   601→      - commoncloud
   602→    labels:
   603→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   604→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=anytype"
   605→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   606→    healthcheck:
   607→      test: ["CMD", "bash", "-c", "(echo > /dev/tcp/localhost/33010) 2>/dev/null || exit 1"]
   608→      interval: 30s
   609→      timeout: 5s
   610→      retries: 3
   611→      start_period: 60s
   612→    security_opt:
   613→      - no-new-privileges:true
   614→    deploy:
   615→      resources:
   616→        limits:
   617→          memory: 1G
   618→          cpus: '1.0'
   619→        reservations:
   620→          memory: 256M
   621→
   622→  # ===========================================
   623→  # SNIKKET - XMPP Messaging
   624→  # snikket.cloudpublica.org
   625→  # ===========================================
   626→  snikket:
   627→    image: snikket/snikket-server:stable
   628→    container_name: snikket
   629→    restart: unless-stopped
   630→    ports:
   631→      - "5222:5222"     # XMPP client connections
   632→      - "5269:5269"     # XMPP server federation
   633→      - "5000:5000"     # File transfer proxy
   634→      - "3478:3478"     # STUN/TURN TCP
   635→      - "3478:3478/udp" # STUN/TURN UDP
   636→      - "3479:3479"     # STUN/TURN TCP alt
   637→      - "3479:3479/udp" # STUN/TURN UDP alt
   638→      - "5349:5349"     # STUN/TURN TLS
   639→      - "5350:5350"     # STUN/TURN TLS alt
   640→      - "49152-49252:49152-49252/udp"  # Media relay (reduced range)
   641→    environment:
   642→      - SNIKKET_DOMAIN=snikket.cloudpublica.org
   643→      - SNIKKET_ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@cloudpublica.org
   644→      - SNIKKET_TWEAK_TURNSERVER_MIN_PORT=49152
   645→      - SNIKKET_TWEAK_TURNSERVER_MAX_PORT=49252
   646→      - SNIKKET_TWEAK_PORTAL_ENABLE=1
   647→      - SNIKKET_TWEAK_INTERNAL_HTTP_INTERFACE=0.0.0.0
   648→    volumes:
   649→      - snikket_data:/snikket
   650→      - ./snikket-certs:/snikket/letsencrypt:ro
   651→    networks:
   652→      - commoncloud
   653→    labels:
   654→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   655→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=snikket"
   656→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   657→    healthcheck:
   658→      test: ["CMD", "bash", "-c", "(echo > /dev/tcp/localhost/5222) 2>/dev/null || exit 1"]
   659→      interval: 30s
   660→      timeout: 5s
   661→      retries: 3
   662→      start_period: 120s
   663→    deploy:
   664→      resources:
   665→        limits:
   666→          memory: 1G
   667→          cpus: '1.0'
   668→        reservations:
   669→          memory: 256M
   670→
   671→  # ===========================================
   672→  # SNIKKET WEB PORTAL - Admin interface
   673→  # snikket.cloudpublica.org (invite management, user admin)
   674→  # ===========================================
   675→  snikket-portal:
   676→    image: snikket/snikket-web-portal:stable
   677→    container_name: snikket-portal
   678→    restart: unless-stopped
   679→    environment:
   680→      - SNIKKET_DOMAIN=snikket.cloudpublica.org
   681→      - SNIKKET_WEB_PROSODY_ENDPOINT=http://snikket:5280/
   682→      - SNIKKET_TWEAK_PORTAL_INTERNAL_HTTP_INTERFACE=0.0.0.0
   683→      - SNIKKET_TWEAK_PORTAL_INTERNAL_HTTP_PORT=5765
   684→    networks:
   685→      - commoncloud
   686→    depends_on:
   687→      - snikket
   688→    labels:
   689→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   690→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=snikket-portal"
   691→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   692→    security_opt:
   693→      - no-new-privileges:true
   694→    deploy:
   695→      resources:
   696→        limits:
   697→          memory: 256M
   698→          cpus: '0.5'
   699→        reservations:
   700→          memory: 64M
   701→
   702→  # ===========================================
   703→  # LINKSTACK MCP - HTTP MCP Server for LinkStack
   704→  # linkstack-mcp.cloudpublica.org
   705→  # ===========================================
   706→  linkstack-mcp:
   707→    build:
   708→      context: ./linkstack-mcp-server
   709→      dockerfile: Dockerfile
   710→    container_name: linkstack-mcp
   711→    restart: unless-stopped
   712→    environment:
   713→      - LINKSTACK_DB_PATH=/data/linkstack/database.sqlite
   714→      # API key set at deployment time (clients fetch from 1Password and pass in Authorization header)
   715→      - LINKSTACK_MCP_API_KEY=${LINKSTACK_MCP_API_KEY}
   716→      - PORT=3000
   717→    volumes:
   718→      # Database mounted via SSHFS from YunoHost server
   719→      # Setup: Run setup-sshfs.sh on Docker server, then mount host path
   720→      - /mnt/linkstack-db/database.sqlite:/data/linkstack/database.sqlite:ro
   721→    networks:
   722→      - commoncloud
   723→    labels:
   724→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.env=production"
   725→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.service=linkstack-mcp"
   726→      - "com.datadoghq.tags.team=commoncloud"
   727→    healthcheck:
   728→      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
   729→      interval: 30s
   730→      timeout: 10s
   731→      retries: 3
   732→    security_opt:
   733→      - no-new-privileges:true
   734→    deploy:
   735→      resources:
   736→        limits:
   737→          memory: 256M
   738→          cpus: '0.5'
   739→        reservations:
   740→          memory: 64M
   741→
   742→  # ===========================================
   743→  # BTCPAY SERVER - Cryptocurrency Payments
   744→  # btc.commoncloud.cc (Optional - for crypto payments)
   745→  # ===========================================
   746→  # btcpay:
   747→  #   image: btcpayserver/btcpayserver:latest
   748→  #   container_name: btcpay
   749→  #   restart: unless-stopped
   750→  #   environment:
   751→  #     - BTCPAY_HOST=btc.commoncloud.cc
   752→  #     - BTCPAY_ROOTPATH=/
   753→  #     - NBITCOIN_NETWORK=mainnet
   754→  #     - BTCPAY_POSTGRES=Host=btcpay-postgres;Database=btcpay;Username=btcpay;Password=${BTCPAY_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
   755→  #   volumes:
   756→  #     - btcpay_data:/datadir
   757→  #   ports:
   758→  #     - "23000:23000"
   759→  #   depends_on:
   760→  #     - btcpay-postgres
   761→  #   networks:
   762→  #     - commoncloud
   763→  #
   764→  # btcpay-postgres:
   765→  #   image: postgres:13-alpine
   766→  #   container_name: btcpay-postgres
   767→  #   restart: unless-stopped
   768→  #   environment:
   769→  #     - POSTGRES_DB=btcpay
   770→  #     - POSTGRES_USER=btcpay
   771→  #     - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${BTCPAY_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
   772→  #   volumes:
   773→  #     - btcpay_postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   774→  #   networks:
   775→  #     - commoncloud
   776→
   777→# ===========================================
   778→# NETWORKS
   779→# ===========================================
   780→networks:
   781→  commoncloud:
   782→    driver: bridge
   783→
   784→# ===========================================
   785→# VOLUMES
   786→# ===========================================
   787→volumes:
   788→  appsmith_data:
   789→    driver: local
   790→  n8n_data:
   791→    driver: local
   792→  statamic_data:
   793→    driver: local
   794→  redis_data:
   795→    driver: local
   796→  billing_db_data:
   797→    driver: local
   798→  splunk_data:
   799→    driver: local
   800→  splunk_etc:
   801→    driver: local
   802→  # AppFlowy volumes
   803→  appflowy_postgres_data:
   804→    driver: local
   805→  appflowy_minio_data:
   806→    driver: local
   807→  appflowy_redis_data:
   808→    driver: local
   809→  # Anytype volume
   810→  anytype_data:
   811→    driver: local
   812→  # Snikket volume
   813→  snikket_data:

   815→  # LinkStack MCP volume (for database access)
   816→  linkstack_db:
   817→    driver: local
   818→  # Uncomment for BTCPay:

data.prompt:
Find and explore the commoncloud.git repository, specifically the 04-docker-server deployment pattern. Look for:
1. Any repo named "commoncloud" in the user's filesystem - check ~/Library/CloudStorage/, ~/, /tmp/, etc.
2. The GitHub Actions workflow files that deploy to AWS Lightsail
3. The deployment configuration in the 04-docker-server directory/branch
4. Understand the deployment pipeline: how does it deploy static sites to the VM?

Also check the GitHub repo at Gifted-Dreamers/commoncloud.cc using the gh CLI or MCP tools if available. Look at the 04-docker-server branch or directory for the deployment workflow.

content.content:
1→# Docker Server Setup
     2→
     3→<!--
     4→================================================================================
     5→CLAUDE NOTES - READ BEFORE PROCEEDING
     6→================================================================================
     7→DEPLOYMENT STATUS: ✅ COMPLETED (December 20, 2025, updated January 16, 2026)
     8→
     9→DEPLOYED TO: aws-docker server (cloudpublica.org domain)
    10→- Appsmith: https://hq.cloudpublica.org ✅ Running
    11→- n8n: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org ✅ Running
    12→- Statamic: https://base.cloudpublica.org ✅ Running
    13→- SSL: Let's Encrypt certificates installed, expires March 20, 2026
    14→
    15→BEFORE MAKING CHANGES:
    16→1. SSH to server: ssh aws-docker
    17→2. Check status: cd ~/commoncloud && sudo docker compose ps
    18→3. Review logs before changes: sudo docker compose logs [service]
    19→
    20→PRIVACY BY DESIGN REQUIREMENTS:
    21→- All services use HTTPS only (HTTP redirects to HTTPS)
    22→- Email config uses Migadu (privacy-focused provider)
    23→- No analytics/telemetry enabled by default
    24→- Encryption keys generated locally, not shared
    25→
    26→SECURITY HARDENING STATUS:
    27→- [x] TLS 1.2+ only with strong ciphers (ssl_protocols in nginx.conf)
    28→- [x] HTTPS enforced (HTTP→HTTPS redirect)
    29→- [x] Reverse proxy isolates containers from direct access
    30→- [x] Rate limiting on nginx (general, login, api zones)
    31→- [x] Security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection)
    32→- [x] Container resource limits (memory, CPU)
    33→- [x] security_opt: no-new-privileges on all containers
    34→- [x] Health checks on all containers
    35→- [x] Fail2ban configuration documented (Step 10)
    36→- [x] Automatic security updates documented (Step 11)
    37→
    38→AFTER DEPLOYMENT - APPLY ON SERVER:
    39→- [ ] Install Fail2ban (see Step 10)
    40→- [ ] Configure unattended-upgrades (see Step 11)
    41→- [ ] Enable HSTS header (uncomment in nginx.conf after SSL verified)
    42→
    43→ALIGNMENT CHECK:
    44→- Verify changes align with ARCHITECTURE.md network diagram
    45→- Ensure new services follow same security patterns
    46→- Update this file when deployment changes
    47→================================================================================
    48→-->
    49→
    50→## Overview
    51→
    52→This guide installs Docker and deploys:
    53→- **Appsmith** (hq.cloudpublica.org) - Community portal
    54→- **n8n** (n8n.cloudpublica.org) - Workflow automation
    55→- **Statamic** (base.cloudpublica.org) - Knowledge base CMS
    56→
    57→Estimated time: 30 minutes
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## Prerequisites
    62→
    63→- [ ] Docker server running (from Step 1)
    64→- [ ] DNS records configured (from Step 2)
    65→- [ ] SSH key file: `~/.ssh/commoncloud-docker.pem`
    66→- [ ] 1Password CLI installed and authenticated (see README.md)
    67→
    68→### 1Password CLI Credentials
    69→
    70→All secrets for Docker services are stored in 1Password. Retrieve them with:
    71→
    72→```bash
    73→# Docker Hub credentials (for private images)
    74→op read "op://Engineering/Docker Hub API Credentials/username"
    75→op read "op://Engineering/Docker Hub API Credentials/credential"
    76→
    77→# Migadu SMTP (for email sending from n8n, Appsmith)
    78→op read "op://Engineering/Migadu API Credentials/username"
    79→op read "op://Engineering/Migadu API Credentials/password"
    80→
    81→# n8n API credentials
    82→op read "op://Engineering/n8n Admin cloudpublica.org API Credentials/credential"
    83→```
    84→
    85→---
    86→
    87→## Step 1: Connect to Server
    88→
    89→```bash
    90→# Replace YOUR_DOCKER_IP with actual IP
    91→ssh -i ~/.ssh/commoncloud-docker.pem admin@YOUR_DOCKER_IP
    92→```
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Step 2: Install Docker
    97→
    98→Run the installation script:
    99→
   100→```bash
   101→# Update system
   102→sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
   103→
   104→# Install Docker
   105→curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sudo sh
   106→
   107→# Add current user to docker group
   108→sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
   109→
   110→# Install Docker Compose plugin
   111→sudo apt install docker-compose-plugin -y
   112→
   113→# Log out and back in for group changes
   114→exit
   115→```
   116→
   117→Reconnect:
   118→```bash
   119→ssh -i ~/.ssh/commoncloud-docker.pem admin@YOUR_DOCKER_IP
   120→```
   121→
   122→Verify:
   123→```bash
   124→docker --version
   125→docker compose version
   126→```
   127→
   128→---
   129→
   130→## Step 3: Create Project Directory
   131→
   132→```bash
   133→# Create directory structure
   134→mkdir -p ~/commoncloud/{appsmith,n8n,statamic,nginx/certs,nginx/conf.d}
   135→cd ~/commoncloud
   136→```
   137→
   138→---
   139→
   140→## Step 4: Create Docker Compose File
   141→
   142→```bash
   143→nano docker-compose.yml
   144→```
   145→
   146→Paste the contents of the `docker-compose.yml` file from this folder (provided separately).
   147→
   148→Save: `Ctrl+O`, Enter, `Ctrl+X`
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## Step 5: Create Nginx Configuration
   153→
   154→### Main Nginx Config
   155→
   156→```bash
   157→nano nginx/nginx.conf
   158→```
   159→
   160→Paste:
   161→```nginx
   162→events {
   163→    worker_connections 1024;
   164→}
   165→
   166→http {
   167→    # Logging
   168→    access_log /var/log/nginx/access.log;
   169→    error_log /var/log/nginx/error.log;
   170→
   171→    # Gzip compression
   172→    gzip on;
   173→    gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json application/javascript;
   174→
   175→    # ===========================================
   176→    # RATE LIMITING - Brute Force Protection
   177→    # ===========================================
   178→    # Zone: 10MB shared memory (~160,000 IP addresses)
   179→    # Rate: 10 requests/second per IP (burst allows short spikes)
   180→    limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=general:10m rate=10r/s;
   181→    limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=login:10m rate=5r/m;
   182→    limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=api:10m rate=30r/s;
   183→
   184→    # Return 429 Too Many Requests (not 503)
   185→    limit_req_status 429;
   186→
   187→    # Log rate limit violations
   188→    limit_req_log_level warn;
   189→
   190→    # ===========================================
   191→    # SECURITY HEADERS
   192→    # ===========================================
   193→    # Prevent clickjacking
   194→    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
   195→    # Prevent MIME type sniffing
   196→    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
   197→    # Enable XSS filter
   198→    add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;
   199→    # Referrer policy
   200→    add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
   201→
   202→    # ===========================================
   203→    # SSL HARDENING
   204→    # ===========================================
   205→    ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
   206→    ssl_ciphers ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384;
   207→    ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off;
   208→    ssl_session_cache shared:SSL:10m;
   209→    ssl_session_timeout 1d;
   210→    ssl_session_tickets off;
   211→
   212→    # HSTS (uncomment after confirming SSL works)
   213→    # add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000" always;
   214→
   215→    # Include site configs
   216→    include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf;
   217→}
   218→```

   446→## Step 7: Obtain SSL Certificates
   447→
   448→Before starting services, get SSL certificates:
   449→
   450→```bash
   451→# Create certbot directories
   452→mkdir -p ~/commoncloud/certbot/www ~/commoncloud/certbot/conf
   453→
   454→# Start a temporary nginx for certificate validation
   455→docker run -d --name temp-nginx \
   456→  -p 80:80 \
   457→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/www:/var/www/certbot:ro \
   458→  nginx:alpine
   459→
   460→# Get certificates for each domain
   461→docker run --rm \
   462→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/conf:/etc/letsencrypt \
   463→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/www:/var/www/certbot \
   464→  certbot/certbot certonly \
   465→  --webroot -w /var/www/certbot \
   466→  --email admin@cloudpublica.org \
   467→  --agree-tos --no-eff-email \
   468→  -d hq.cloudpublica.org
   469→
   470→docker run --rm \
   471→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/conf:/etc/letsencrypt \
   472→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/www:/var/www/certbot \
   473→  certbot/certbot certonly \
   474→  --webroot -w /var/www/certbot \
   475→  --email admin@cloudpublica.org \
   476→  --agree-tos --no-eff-email \
   477→  -d n8n.cloudpublica.org
   478→
   479→docker run --rm \
   480→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/conf:/etc/letsencrypt \
   481→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/www:/var/www/certbot \
   482→  certbot/certbot certonly \
   483→  --webroot -w /var/www/certbot \
   484→  --email admin@cloudpublica.org \
   485→  --agree-tos --no-eff-email \
   486→  -d base.cloudpublica.org
   487→
   488→# Stop and remove temporary nginx

   514→## Step 9: Initial Application Setup
   515→
   516→### Appsmith (hq.cloudpublica.org)
   517→
   518→#### Step 1: Create Admin Account
   519→
   520→1. Open https://hq.cloudpublica.org
   521→2. Create admin account when prompted
   522→3. Save credentials securely
   523→
   524→#### Step 2: Configure Datasources (Deferred to v1.1)
   525→
   526→> **Note:** Datasource configuration is part of the **billing system (v1.1)** scheduled for Q1 2026.
   527→> Skip this step for now. The datasources below will be configured when implementing billing.
   528→
   529→After logging in, Appsmith will prompt you to set up datasources. When ready for v1.1, configure these connections:
   530→
   531→**1. PostgreSQL - Billing Database**
   532→
   533→| Setting | Value |
   534→|---------|-------|
   535→| Host | `billing-db` |
   536→| Port | `5432` |
   537→| Database | `billing` |
   538→| Username | `billing` |
   539→| Password | (from .env `BILLING_DB_PASSWORD`) |
   540→| SSL Mode | Disable (internal network) |
   541→
   542→**2. REST API - n8n Webhooks**
   543→
   544→| Setting | Value |
   545→|---------|-------|
   546→| Name | `n8n-webhooks` |
   547→| Base URL | `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook` |
   548→| Authentication | None (webhooks are public endpoints) |
   549→
   550→This datasource is used for:
   551→- `POST /stripe` - Stripe payment webhooks
   552→- `POST /create-checkout` - Create Stripe checkout session
   553→- `POST /customer-portal` - Open Stripe customer portal
   554→- `POST /btcpay-invoice` - Create BTCPay invoice
   555→
   556→**3. REST API - YunoHost API (Optional)**
   557→
   558→| Setting | Value |
   559→|---------|-------|
   560→| Name | `yunohost-api` |
   561→| Base URL | `https://commoncloud.cc/yunohost/api` |
   562→| Authentication | Bearer Token |
   563→| Header | `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>` |
   564→
   565→Used for automated user provisioning after payment.
   566→
   567→#### Step 3: Verify Connections
   568→
   569→Test each datasource connection before proceeding. The billing database should return tables from the schema.
   570→

   572→
   573→### Docker Apps Integration Diagram
   574→
   575→```
   576→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   577→│                    Docker Network (commoncloud)                  │
   578→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   579→│                                                                  │
   580→│  ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐    │
   581→│  │   Appsmith   │────▶│   billing-db │     │    redis     │    │
   582→│  │ (hq.cloud..) │     │  (PostgreSQL)│     │   (cache)    │    │
   583→│  └──────┬───────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────────┘    │
   584→│         │                                                        │
   585→│         │ REST API calls                                         │
   586→│         ▼                                                        │
   587→│  ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐                          │
   588→│  │     n8n      │────▶│   Statamic   │                          │
   589→│  │(n8n.cloud..) │     │(base.cloud..)│                          │
   590→│  └──────┬───────┘     └──────────────┘                          │
   591→│         │                                                        │
   592→└─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   593→          │
   594→          │ External API calls
   595→          ▼
   596→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   597→│                     External Services                            │
   598→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   599→│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐          │
   600→│  │    Stripe    │  │   BTCPay     │  │   YunoHost   │          │
   601→│  │  (payments)  │  │   (crypto)   │  │   (users)    │          │
   602→│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘          │
   603→│                                                                  │
   604→│  ┌──────────────┐                                                │
   605→│  │   Migadu     │                                                │
   606→│  │   (email)    │                                                │
   607→│  └──────────────┘                                                │
   608→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   609→```
   610→
   611→**Data Flow:**
   612→1. User signs up via Appsmith form → stored in billing-db
   613→2. Appsmith triggers n8n webhook → n8n creates Stripe checkout
   614→3. Stripe webhook → n8n → updates billing-db → provisions YunoHost user
   615→4. n8n sends welcome email via Migadu SMTP
   616→
   617→### n8n (n8n.cloudpublica.org)
   618→
   619→1. Open https://n8n.cloudpublica.org
   620→2. Log in with credentials from `.env` file
   621→3. Create your first workflow
   622→
   623→### Statamic (base.cloudpublica.org)
   624→
   625→Statamic requires post-deployment configuration before first use.
   626→
   627→#### Step 1: Fix Configuration (Required)
   628→
   629→```bash
   630→# SSH to server
   631→ssh aws-docker
   632→
   633→# Fix APP_URL (default is localhost, must be production URL)
   634→docker exec statamic sed -i 's|APP_URL=http://localhost|APP_URL=https://base.cloudpublica.org|' /var/www/html/.env
   635→docker exec statamic sed -i 's|APP_ENV=local|APP_ENV=production|' /var/www/html/.env
   636→
   637→# Configure trusted proxy (required for HTTPS behind nginx)
   638→docker exec statamic bash -c 'cat > /var/www/html/bootstrap/app.php << '\''EOF'\''
   639→<?php
   640→
   641→use Illuminate\Foundation\Application;
   642→use Illuminate\Foundation\Configuration\Exceptions;
   643→use Illuminate\Foundation\Configuration\Middleware;
   644→
   645→return Application::configure(basePath: dirname(__DIR__))
   646→    ->withRouting(
   647→        web: __DIR__."/../routes/web.php",
   648→        commands: __DIR__."/../routes/console.php",
   649→        health: "/up",
   650→    )
   651→    ->withMiddleware(function (Middleware $middleware): void {
   652→        $middleware->trustProxies(at: "*");
   653→    })
   654→    ->withExceptions(function (Exceptions $exceptions): void {
   655→        //
   656→    })->create();
   657→EOF'
   658→
   659→# Fix permissions (required for user data writes)
   660→docker exec statamic chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/html/users
   661→docker exec statamic chmod -R 775 /var/www/html/users
   662→docker exec statamic chmod -R 777 /var/www/html/storage
   663→
   664→# Publish Control Panel assets
   665→docker exec statamic php artisan vendor:publish --tag=statamic-cp
   666→
   667→# Clear caches and restart
   668→docker exec statamic php artisan config:clear
   669→docker exec statamic php artisan cache:clear
   670→cd ~/commoncloud && docker compose restart statamic
   671→```
   672→
   673→#### Step 2: Create Admin User
   674→
   675→```bash
   676→# Create admin user (replace password with a secure one)
   677→docker exec statamic php please make:user admin@cloudpublica.org --super --password=YOUR_SECURE_PASSWORD -n
   678→```
   679→
   680→#### Step 3: Access Control Panel
   681→
   682→1. Open https://base.cloudpublica.org/cp
   683→2. Log in with the admin credentials
   684→3. Go to **Profile** (top-right avatar) to change password
   685→4. Start building your knowledge base
   686→
   687→---
   688→
   689→### Statamic Troubleshooting
   690→
   691→**Problem: 500 Server Error on login**
   692→- Cause: APP_URL set to localhost, TrustProxies not configured
   693→- Fix: Run Step 1 configuration above
   694→
   695→**Problem: No sidebar navigation / broken CSS**
   696→- Cause: Control Panel assets not published
   697→- Fix: `docker exec statamic php artisan vendor:publish --tag=statamic-cp`
   698→
   699→**Problem: "Permission denied" when saving**
   700→- Cause: File permissions not set for www-data user
   701→- Fix: `docker exec statamic chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/html/users`
   702→
   703→**Problem: Assets loading via HTTP instead of HTTPS (mixed content)**
   704→- Cause: TrustProxies middleware not configured for reverse proxy
   705→- Fix: Update bootstrap/app.php with `$middleware->trustProxies(at: "*")`
   706→
   707→---
   708→
   709→### Statamic Post-Mortem Notes
   710→
   711→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   712→These issues stem from Statamic's default Docker image being configured for local development, not production behind a reverse proxy.
   713→
   714→**Issues encountered (all fixable, not bugs):**
   715→
   716→| Issue | Cause | Source |
   717→|-------|-------|--------|
   718→| 500 error on login | APP_URL=localhost in .env | Default Statamic config |
   719→| Mixed content (HTTP assets) | TrustProxies not configured | Laravel 11 requires explicit proxy config |
   720→| No CP sidebar | Assets not published | Statamic requires manual publish step |
   721→| Permission denied on save | Root-owned files | Docker build runs as root |
   722→
   723→**What NOT to do:**
   724→- Do NOT skip the configuration steps and go straight to user creation
   725→- Do NOT assume the container is production-ready out of the box
   726→- Do NOT try web installer routes (/installer) - Statamic doesn't have one
   727→
   728→**What TO do:**
   729→- Always run the full Step 1 configuration after first deploy
   730→- Always verify APP_URL matches production domain before creating users
   731→- Always publish CP assets before first login
   732→

   735→## Step 10: Set Up Auto-Renewal for SSL
   736→
   737→```bash
   738→# Create renewal script
   739→nano ~/commoncloud/renew-certs.sh
   740→```
   741→
   742→Paste:
   743→```bash
   744→#!/bin/bash
   745→cd ~/commoncloud
   746→docker run --rm \
   747→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/conf:/etc/letsencrypt \
   748→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/www:/var/www/certbot \
   749→  certbot/certbot renew
   750→
   751→cp -rL ~/commoncloud/certbot/conf/live ~/commoncloud/nginx/certs/
   752→docker compose restart nginx
   753→```
   754→
   755→Make executable and schedule:
   756→```bash
   757→chmod +x ~/commoncloud/renew-certs.sh
   758→
   759→# Add to crontab (runs daily at 3am)
   760→(crontab -l 2>/dev/null; echo "0 3 * * * ~/commoncloud/renew-certs.sh") | crontab -
   761→```

   789→
   790→# Stop all services
   791→docker compose down
   792→
   793→# Start all services
   794→docker compose up -d
   795→
   796→# Update images
   797→docker compose pull
   798→docker compose up -d
   799→
   800→# View resource usage
   801→docker stats
   802→```
   803→
   804→---
   805→
   806→## Troubleshooting
   807→
   808→### Container won't start
   809→
   810→```bash
   811→# Check logs
   812→docker compose logs appsmith
   813→
   814→# Check if ports are in use
   815→sudo netstat -tlnp | grep -E '80|443'
   816→```
   817→
   818→### SSL certificate issues
   819→
   820→```bash
   821→# Verify certificates exist
   822→ls -la ~/commoncloud/nginx/certs/live/
   823→
   824→# Re-run certbot manually
   825→docker run -it --rm \
   826→  -v ~/commoncloud/certbot/conf:/etc/letsencrypt \
   827→  certbot/certbot certificates
   828→```
   829→
   830→### Out of disk space
   831→
   832→```bash
   833→# Check disk usage
   834→df -h
   835→
   836→# Clean up Docker
   837→docker system prune -a
   838→```
   839→
   840→---
   841→
   842→## Step 10: Install Fail2ban (Brute Force Protection)
   843→
   844→Fail2ban monitors logs and bans IPs that show malicious signs (failed logins, rate limit violations).
   845→
   846→```bash
   847→# Install fail2ban
   848→sudo apt update && sudo apt install fail2ban -y
   849→
   850→# Create local config (never edit jail.conf directly)
   851→sudo cp /etc/fail2ban/jail.conf /etc/fail2ban/jail.local
   852→```
   853→
   854→Edit the local config:
   855→```bash
   856→sudo nano /etc/fail2ban/jail.local
   857→```
   858→
   859→Add/modify these settings:
   860→```ini
   861→[DEFAULT]
   862→# Ban for 1 hour after 5 failures
   863→bantime = 3600
   864→findtime = 600
   865→maxretry = 5
   866→
   867→# Email notifications (optional - requires mail setup)
   868→# destemail = admin@cloudpublica.org
   869→# sendername = Fail2ban
   870→# mta = sendmail
   871→
   872→[sshd]
   873→enabled = true
   874→port = ssh
   875→logpath = /var/log/auth.log
   876→maxretry = 3
   877→
   878→[nginx-http-auth]
   879→enabled = true
   880→port = http,https
   881→logpath = /var/log/nginx/error.log
   882→
   883→[nginx-limit-req]
   884→enabled = true
   885→port = http,https
   886→logpath = /var/log/nginx/error.log
   887→maxretry = 10
   888→```
   889→
   890→Create a custom filter for nginx rate limiting:
   891→```bash
   892→sudo nano /etc/fail2ban/filter.d/nginx-limit-req.conf
   893→```
   894→
   895→```ini
   896→[Definition]
   897→failregex = limiting requests, excess:.* by zone.*client: <HOST>
   898→ignoreregex =
   899→```
   900→
   901→Start and enable fail2ban:
   902→```bash
   903→sudo systemctl enable fail2ban
   904→sudo systemctl start fail2ban
   905→
   906→# Check status
   907→sudo fail2ban-client status
   908→
   909→# Check specific jail
   910→sudo fail2ban-client status sshd
   911→sudo fail2ban-client status nginx-limit-req
   912→```
   913→
   914→---
   915→
   916→## Step 11: Security Update Schedule
   917→
   918→Set up automatic security updates:
   919→
   920→```bash
   921→# Install unattended-upgrades
   922→sudo apt install unattended-upgrades -y
   923→
   924→# Configure automatic security updates
   925→sudo dpkg-reconfigure -plow unattended-upgrades
   926→```
   927→
   928→Create a cron job for Docker image updates (weekly):
   929→```bash
   930→sudo crontab -e
   931→```
   932→
   933→Add:
   934→```cron
   935→# Update Docker images weekly (Sunday 3am)
   936→0 3 * * 0 cd /home/admin/commoncloud && docker compose pull && docker compose up -d
   937→```
   938→
   939→---
   940→
   941→## Next Step
   942→
   943→Before proceeding to the next phase:
   944→
   945→1. **Security Audit** - Review the security hardening checklist at the top of this file
   946→2. **Verify deployment** - Confirm all services are accessible and functioning
   947→3. **Test Fail2ban** - Verify fail2ban is running: `sudo fail2ban-client status`
   948→
   949→Then proceed to **[05-monitoring/](../05-monitoring/)** to set up Datadog, Splunk, and PagerDuty (optional for v1.0).
   950→
   951→---
   952→
   953→## Billing Database (Phase 8)
   954→
   955→The Docker Compose file includes a PostgreSQL database for billing. To enable it:
   956→
   957→### 1. Add Environment Variables
   958→
   959→Add to `~/commoncloud/.env`:
   960→
   961→```bash
   962→# Billing Database
   963→# Store in 1Password: op item create --category=password --title="Billing DB Password" --vault=Engineering password=[password]
   964→BILLING_DB_PASSWORD=your_secure_password_here
   965→
   966→# Stripe (get from stripe.com/dashboard)
   967→# Store in 1Password after creating: op item create --category="API Credential" --title="Stripe API Credentials" --vault=Engineering
   968→STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_...
   969→STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...
   970→STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_live_...
   971→
   972→# BTCPay (optional - for crypto payments)
   973→BTCPAY_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=your_btcpay_password
   974→```
   975→
   976→### 2. Copy Schema File
   977→
   978→```bash
   979→cp /path/to/08-billing/schema.sql ~/commoncloud/billing-schema.sql
   980→```
   981→
   982→### 3. Start Database
   983→
   984→```bash
   985→cd ~/commoncloud
   986→docker compose up -d billing-db
   987→
   988→# Verify it's running
   989→docker compose ps billing-db
   990→docker compose logs billing-db
   991→```
   992→
   993→### 4. Test Connection
   994→
   995→```bash
   996→docker exec -it billing-db psql -U billing -d billing -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;"
   997→```
   998→
   999→See **[08-billing/](../08-billing/)** for complete billing system setup.
  1000→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→# ===========================================
     2→# Common Cloud - Validation Workflow
     3→# ===========================================
     4→# Validates Terraform, Docker Compose, and shell scripts
     5→# Runs on every push and pull request
     6→
     7→name: Validate
     8→
     9→on:
    10→  push:
    11→    branches: [main, develop]
    12→  pull_request:
    13→    branches: [main, develop]
    14→
    15→jobs:
    16→  # ===========================================
    17→  # Terraform Validation
    18→  # ===========================================
    19→  terraform:
    20→    name: Terraform
    21→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    22→    permissions:
    23→      contents: read
    24→    defaults:
    25→      run:
    26→        working-directory: 00-terraform
    27→
    28→    steps:
    29→      - name: Checkout code
    30→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    31→
    32→      - name: Setup Terraform
    33→        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
    34→        with:
    35→          terraform_version: "1.6.0"
    36→
    37→      - name: Terraform Format Check
    38→        id: fmt
    39→        run: terraform fmt -check -recursive
    40→        continue-on-error: true
    41→
    42→      - name: Terraform Init
    43→        id: init
    44→        run: terraform init -backend=false
    45→
    46→      - name: Terraform Validate
    47→        id: validate
    48→        run: terraform validate
    49→
    50→      - name: Post validation status
    51→        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    52→        continue-on-error: true
    53→        run: |
    54→          echo "### Terraform Validation Results"
    55→          echo ""
    56→          echo "| Check | Status |"
    57→          echo "|-------|--------|"
    58→          echo "| Format | ${{ steps.fmt.outcome == 'success' && '✅ Passed' || '⚠️ Needs formatting' }} |"
    59→          echo "| Init | ${{ steps.init.outcome == 'success' && '✅ Passed' || '❌ Failed' }} |"
    60→          echo "| Validate | ${{ steps.validate.outcome == 'success' && '✅ Passed' || '❌ Failed' }} |"
    61→
    62→  # ===========================================
    63→  # Docker Compose Validation
    64→  # ===========================================
    65→  docker:
    66→    name: Docker Compose
    67→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    68→    permissions:
    69→      contents: read
    70→
    71→    steps:
    72→      - name: Checkout code
    73→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    74→
    75→      - name: Create test .env file
    76→        run: |
    77→          cat > 04-docker-server/.env << 'EOF'
    78→          # Test environment variables for CI
    79→          N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER=test
    80→          N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD=test
    81→          N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY=test-key-32-characters-long-xxx
    82→          STATAMIC_APP_KEY=base64:test-key-for-ci-validation
    83→          BILLING_DB_PASSWORD=test
    84→          TZ=UTC
    85→          EOF
    86→
    87→      - name: Validate docker-compose.yml
    88→        run: |
    89→          cd 04-docker-server
    90→          docker compose config --quiet
    91→          echo "✅ docker-compose.yml is valid"
    92→
    93→      - name: Check for latest tag usage
    94→        run: |
    95→          cd 04-docker-server
    96→          if grep -q ":latest" docker-compose.yml; then
    97→            echo "⚠️ Warning: Using ':latest' tags. Consider pinning versions for production."
    98→          fi
    99→
   100→  # ===========================================
   101→  # Shell Script Linting
   102→  # ===========================================
   103→  shellcheck:
   104→    name: Shell Scripts
   105→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   106→    permissions:
   107→      contents: read
   108→
   109→    steps:
   110→      - name: Checkout code
   111→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
   112→
   113→      - name: Run ShellCheck
   114→        uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master
   115→        with:
   116→          scandir: "."
   117→          severity: warning
   118→
   119→  # ===========================================
   120→  # YAML Linting
   121→  # ===========================================
   122→  yaml-lint:
   123→    name: YAML Files
   124→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   125→    permissions:
   126→      contents: read
   127→
   128→    steps:
   129→      - name: Checkout code
   130→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
   131→
   132→      - name: Lint YAML files
   133→        uses: ibiqlik/action-yamllint@v3
   134→        with:
   135→          file_or_dir: .
   136→          config_data: |
   137→            extends: relaxed
   138→            rules:
   139→              line-length:
   140→                max: 150
   141→                level: warning
   142→              truthy:
   143→                check-keys: false
   144→
   145→  # ===========================================
   146→  # Makefile Validation
   147→  # ===========================================
   148→  makefile:
   149→    name: Makefile
   150→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   151→    permissions:
   152→      contents: read
   153→
   154→    steps:
   155→      - name: Checkout code
   156→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
   157→
   158→      - name: Validate Makefile syntax
   159→        run: |
   160→          make -n help > /dev/null 2>&1
   161→          echo "✅ Makefile syntax is valid"
   162→
   163→      - name: List available targets
   164→        run: |
   165→          echo "Available make targets:"
   166→          make help || true
   167→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→# Docker server environment variables
     2→
     3→**No secrets in this repo.** All values are set on the server only (e.g. `~/commoncloud/.env`). Use 1Password or generate locally and paste on the server.
     4→
     5→Variable names required by `docker-compose.yml`:
     6→
     7→| Variable | Where to set / get |
     8→|----------|--------------------|
     9→| **Domains** | |
    10→| APPSMITH_DOMAIN | hq.cloudpublica.org |
    11→| N8N_DOMAIN | n8n.cloudpublica.org |
    12→| STATAMIC_DOMAIN | base.cloudpublica.org |
    13→| ADMIN_EMAIL | e.g. ccadmin@cloudpublica.org (Let's Encrypt) |

    15→| **n8n** | |
    16→| N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER | Admin username |

    18→| N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY | `openssl rand -hex 32` (or use generate-keys.sh) |

content.content:
1→# Common Cloud Deployment Package
     2→
     3→> **For detailed infrastructure info (IPs, credentials, MCPs, APIs):** See [docs/INFRASTRUCTURE.md](docs/INFRASTRUCTURE.md)
     4→>
     5→> **For current tasks:** See [docs/TODO.md](docs/TODO.md)
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→This package contains everything needed to deploy the Common Cloud infrastructure. V1 deployment by Gifted Dreamers Inc, 501(c)(3), a nonprofit based in Austin, Texas. Common Cloud is an open source, sovereign, FOSS-based cloud tech stack, with core principles of privacy-by-design that resists wealth extraction, surveillance, deplatforming, and hierarchical control. Powered by People. Community-owned by diverse & multi-lingual users across the digital divide. Federate the Common Cloud and expand the grid of infrastructure for community-led resilience.
    10→
    11→| Server | Domain | Provider | Status |
    12→|--------|--------|----------|--------|
    13→| VM 1: YunoHost | thecommoncloud.org | GCP | Legacy |
    14→| VM 2: YunoHost | commoncloud.cc | AWS Lightsail | ✅ Active (Primary) |
    15→| VM 3: Docker | cloudpublica.org | AWS Lightsail | ✅ Active |
    16→| Email Relay | Migadu.com | Swiss hosting | ✅ Active |
    17→
    18→**Full server details:** [docs/INFRASTRUCTURE.md](docs/INFRASTRUCTURE.md)
    19→
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Architecture Summary
    24→
    25→### VM 1: YunoHost (`thecommoncloud.org`) - Legacy/Secondary
    26→
    27→| Subdomain | App | Purpose |
    28→|-----------|-----|---------|
    29→| thecommoncloud.org | YunoHost Portal | SSO login, user management |
    30→| matrix.thecommoncloud.org | Synapse + Element | Encrypted messaging |
    31→| thecommoncloud.org/nextcloud | Nextcloud | File storage & sync |
    32→| meet.thecommoncloud.org | Jitsi Meet | Video conferencing |
    33→| vault.thecommoncloud.org | Vaultwarden | Password management |
    34→| pad.thecommoncloud.org | Cryptpad | Collaborative documents |
    35→| maps.thecommoncloud.org | Facilmap | Collaborative maps |
    36→| mail.thecommoncloud.org | (legacy) | Webmail (GCP server) |
    37→| guard.thecommoncloud.org | AdGuard Home | DNS filtering |
    38→| vpn.thecommoncloud.org | WireGuard | VPN server |
    39→| sync.thecommoncloud.org | Syncthing | P2P file synchronization |
    40→| mesh.thecommoncloud.org | Mosquitto | MQTT broker for Meshtastic/IoT |
    41→| library.thecommoncloud.org | Kiwix | Offline content library |
    42→
    43→### VM 2: YunoHost (`commoncloud.cc`) - Primary ✅
    44→
    45→**Onion (Tor):** [nwhm4z34ozhium4lj4hgub5d7l3dlutnmv3zrxukjerdo2qpvicnw5ad.onion](https://nwhm4z34ozhium4lj4hgub5d7l3dlutnmv3zrxukjerdo2qpvicnw5ad.onion) — same apps as clearnet. **Full app list with URLs:** [docs/HOSTED-APPS.md](docs/HOSTED-APPS.md)
    46→
    47→| Subdomain | App | Purpose | Status |
    48→|-----------|-----|---------|--------|
    49→| commoncloud.cc | YunoHost Portal + mywebapp | SSO login; root website (mywebapp, not yet built; Postgres DB) | ✅ Installed |
    50→| element.commoncloud.cc | Element | Matrix client | ✅ Installed |
    51→| matrix.commoncloud.cc | Synapse | Matrix homeserver (federation) | ✅ Installed |
    52→| cloud.commoncloud.cc | Nextcloud (incl. Mail app) | File storage, sync, email (IMAP/SMTP via Migadu) | ✅ Installed |
    53→| meet.commoncloud.cc | Jitsi Meet | Video conferencing | ✅ Installed |
    54→| vault.commoncloud.cc | Vaultwarden | Password management (admin: /admin; fix //admin in YunoHost: VAULTWARDEN-SUBDOMAIN-FIX.md) | ✅ Installed |
    55→| pad.commoncloud.cc | Cryptpad | Collaborative documents | ✅ Installed |
    56→| office.commoncloud.cc | Collabora Online | Document editing (Nextcloud integration) | ✅ Installed |
    57→| sync.commoncloud.cc | Syncthing | P2P file synchronization | ✅ Installed |
    58→| mesh.commoncloud.cc | Mosquitto | MQTT broker for Meshtastic/IoT | ✅ Installed |
    59→| guard.commoncloud.cc | AdGuard Home | DNS filtering | ✅ Installed |
    60→| vpn.commoncloud.cc | WireGuard | VPN server | ✅ Installed |
    61→| maps.commoncloud.cc | Facilmap | Collaborative maps | ✅ Installed |
    62→| links.commoncloud.cc | LinkStack | Link-in-bio / link aggregator | ✅ Installed |
    63→| library.commoncloud.cc | Kiwix | Offline content library | ✅ Installed |
    64→
    65→### VM 3: Docker (`cloudpublica.org`)
    66→
    67→| Subdomain | App | Purpose | Status |
    68→|-----------|-----|---------|--------|
    69→| gifteddreamers.org | Gifted Dreamers | Main website | ✅ Running |
    70→| hq.cloudpublica.org | Appsmith | Community portal / admin dashboard | ✅ Running |
    71→| n8n.cloudpublica.org | n8n | Workflow automation | ✅ Running |
    72→| base.cloudpublica.org | Statamic | Knowledge base / documentation CMS | ✅ Running |
    73→| logs.cloudpublica.org | Splunk | Log analysis / SIEM | ✅ Running |
    74→| vvveb.cloudpublica.org | VVVeb | Website builder | ✅ Running |
    75→| anytype.cloudpublica.org | Anytype | Sync server (knowledge management) | ✅ Running |
    76→| snikket.cloudpublica.org | Snikket | XMPP messaging | ✅ Running |
    77→| appflowy.cloudpublica.org | AppFlowy Cloud | Notes/docs sync | ✅ Running |
    78→
    79→**Full app list with URLs and GitHub:** [docs/HOSTED-APPS.md](docs/HOSTED-APPS.md)
    80→
    81→### Email Architecture (Migadu SMTP Relay)
    82→
    83→**Why Migadu?** AWS, GCP, and most cloud providers block port 25 (SMTP) to prevent spam. YunoHost's built-in email server won't work without a relay.
    84→
    85→| Component | Purpose |
    86→|-----------|---------|
    87→| Migadu.com | Swiss-hosted SMTP relay, handles sending/receiving |
    88→| YunoHost | Uses Migadu as relay; manages user mailboxes |
    89→| Nextcloud Mail | In-app mail (IMAP/SMTP via Migadu) |
    90→
    91→**Migadu Auto-Creates Emails:** When adding a domain to Migadu, it automatically creates `admin@<domain>` with alias `postmaster@<domain>`. You must manually set the password in Migadu admin.
    92→
    93→**Email Separation Strategy:**
    94→
    95→| Email Type | Purpose | Visibility |
    96→|------------|---------|------------|
    97→| `admin@domain` | Public interactions, user support | Public |
    98→| `ccadmin@domain` | Server admin, SMTP relay authentication | Internal Only |
    99→
   100→**Email addresses requiring configuration:**
